(\n21\t\t\"errors\"\n22\t\t\"fmt\"\n23\t\t\"os\"\n24\t\t\"strings\"\n25\t)\n26\t\n27\t// YAMLError is the base interface for all YAML processing errors.\n28\t//\n29\t// This interface provides a common type for all errors that occur during\n30\t// YAML file I/O, parsing, validation, and schema operations.\n31\ttype YAMLError interface {\n32\t\terror\n33\t\n34\t\t// Code returns the error code (\n48\t\tErrorTypeFile           ErrorType = \"file\"           // File I/O errors\n49\t\tErrorTypeParse          ErrorType = \"parse\"          // YAML parsing errors\n50\t\tErrorTypeSyntax         ErrorType = \"syntax\"         // YAML syntax errors\n51\t\tErrorTypeStructure      ErrorType = \"structure\"      // YAML structure errors\n52\t\tErrorTypeTypeMismatch   ErrorType = \"type_mismatch\" fields\n55\t\tErrorTypeConstraint     ErrorType = \"constraint\"     // Constraint violations\n56\t\tErrorTypeDuplicateKey   ErrorType = \"duplicate_key\"  // Duplicate key type\n61\t\tErrorTypeEmpty          ErrorType = \"empty\"          // Empty content\n62\t\tErrorTypeIO             ErrorType = \"io\"             // I/O error\n63\t)\n64\t\n65\t// ParseErrorVariant represents the specific variant/category of a parse error.\n66\t//\n67\t// ParseErrorVariant provides a structured way to categorize different types of\n68\t// parse errors, enabling precise error handling and user-friendly error messages.\n69\t// Each variant represents a distinct category of parsing failure.\n70\ttype ParseErrorVariant string\n71\t\n72\tconst (\n73\t\t// ParseErrorVariantSyntax represents YAML syntax errors.\n74\t\t//\n75\t\t// This variant covers fundamental YAML syntax issues including:\n76\t\t// - Invalid indentation\n77\t\t// - Malformed YAML structure\n78\t\t// - Invalid escape sequences\n79\t\t// - Improper quoting\n80\t\t// - Invalid YAML tokens\n81\t\tParseErrorVariantSyntax ParseErrorVariant = \"syntax\"\n82\t\n83\t\t// ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch represents type conversion errors.\n84\t\t//\n85\t\t// This variant covers errors when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n86\t\t// expected Go type, including:\n87\t\t// - Attempting to parse a string as an integer\n88\t\t// - Trying to unmarshal a scalar into a struct\n89\t\t// - Incompatible array/slice types\n90\t\t// - Invalid boolean representations\n91\t\tParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch ParseErrorVariant = \"type_mismatch\"\n92\t\n93\t\t// ParseErrorVariantValidation represents constraint validation errors.\n94\t\t//\n95\t\t// This variant covers errors when YAML data violates defined constraints,\n96\t\t// including:\n97\t\t// - Value out of allowed range\n98\t\t// - String length violations\n99\t\t// - Pattern matching failures\n100\t\t// - Required field violations\n101\t\tParseErrorVariantValidation ParseErrorVariant = \"validation\"\n102\t\n103\t\t// ParseErrorVariantIO represents I/O operation errors.\n104\t\t//\n105\t\t// This variant covers errors related to file operations during parsing,\n106\t\t// including:\n107\t\t// - File not found\n108\t\t// - Permission denied\n109\t\t// - Read/write failures\n110\t\t// - File system errors\n111\t\tParseErrorVariantIO ParseErrorVariant = \"io\"\n112\t\n113\t\t// ParseErrorVariantStructure represents YAML structure errors.\n114\t\t//\n115\t\t// This variant covers structural issues that syntax checking alone won't catch,\n116\t\t// including:\n117\t\t// - Duplicate mapping keys\n118\t\t// - Invalid nesting\n119\t\t// - Circular references\n120\t\t// - Inconsistent document structure\n121\t\tParseErrorVariantStructure ParseErrorVariant = \"structure\"\n122\t\n123\t\t// ParseErrorVariantCustom represents custom/extensible error categories.\n124\t\t//\n125\t\t// This variant is reserved for application-specific parsing errors that don't\n126\t\t// fit into the standard categories. It enables extensibility for domain-specific\n127\t\t// validation rules and custom parsing logic.\n128\t\t// Applications can use this variant with custom error codes and messages\n129\t\t// to represent specialized error conditions.\n130\t\tParseErrorVariantCustom ParseErrorVariant = \"custom\"\n131\t)\n132\t\n133\t// String returns the string representation of the ParseErrorVariant.\n134\tfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) String() string {\n135\t\treturn string(v)\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t// Description returns a human-readable description of the ParseErrorVariant.\n139\tfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) Description() string {\n140\t\tswitch v {\n141\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantSyntax:\n142\t\t\treturn \"YAML syntax error - invalid YAML structure or formatting\"\n143\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch:\n144\t\t\treturn \"Type mismatch error - cannot convert YAML to expected type\"\n145\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantValidation:\n146\t\t\treturn \"Validation error - constraint violation or invalid value\"\n147\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantIO:\n148\t\t\treturn \"I/O error - file operation failed\"\n149\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantStructure:\n150\t\t\treturn \"Structure error - invalid YAML document structure\"\n151\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantCustom:\n152\t\t\treturn \"Custom error - application-specific parsing error\"\n153\t\tdefault:\n154\t\t\treturn \"Unknown parse error variant\"\n155\t\t}\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\t// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n159\t//\n160\t// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n161\t// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\n162\ttype ErrorCode string\n163\t\n164\tconst (\n165\t\t// ============================================================================\n166\t\t// File Error Codes\n167\t\t// ============================================================================\n168\t\n169\t\t// ErrCodeFileNotFound indicates that a file does not exist at the specified path.\n170\t\t// This error is returned when file operations fail because the target file cannot\n171\t\t// be found in the filesystem.\n172\t\tErrCodeFileNotFound ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"\n173\t\n174\t\t// ErrCodeFileAccessDenied indicates insufficient permissions to access a file.\n175\t\t// This error occurs when the application lacks the necessary read or write\n176\t\t// permissions for the specified file path.\n177\t\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\"\n178\t\n179\t\t// ErrCodeFileIOError indicates a generic file I/O error that doesn't fit\n180\t\t// other specific file error categories. This includes disk errors, filesystem\n181\t\t// issues, or other low-level I/O failures.\n182\t\tErrCodeFileIOError ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"\n183\t\n184\t\t// ErrCodeFileEmpty indicates that a file exists but contains no data or\n185\t\t// only whitespace. This error is used when parsing an empty file would result\n186\t\t// in undefined behavior or missing required configuration.\n187\t\tErrCodeFileEmpty ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"\n188\t\n189\t\t// ============================================================================\n190\t\t// Parse Error Codes\n191\t\t// ============================================================================\n192\t\n193\t\t// ErrCodeInvalidSyntax indicates a YAML syntax error during parsing.\n194\t\t// This includes invalid indentation, malformed YAML structure, invalid escape\n195\t\t// sequences, improper quoting, or any fundamental YAML syntax violation that\n196\t\t// prevents the document from being parsed.\n197\t\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"\n198\t\n199\t\t// ErrCodeTypeMismatch indicates a type conversion error during parsing.\n200\t\t// This occurs when YAML content cannot be converted to the expected Go type,\n201\t\t// such as attempting to parse a string as an integer, unmarshaling a scalar into\n202\t\t// a struct, or incompatible array/slice types.\n203\t\tErrCodeTypeMismatch ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"\n204\t\n205\t\t// ErrCodeInvalidStructure indicates a YAML structure error that passes\n206\t\t// syntax checking but represents invalid document structure. This includes\n207\t\t// invalid nesting, inconsistent document structure, or other structural issues\n208\t\t// that prevent proper interpretation of the YAML data.\n209\t\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\"\n210\t\n211\t\t// ErrCodeDuplicateKey indicates that a YAML mapping contains the same key\n212\t\t// multiple times. This is ambiguous and often indicates a configuration error,\n213\t\t// as YAML mappings should have unique keys.\n214\t\tErrCodeDuplicateKey ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"\n215\t\n216\t\t// ErrCodeParseError indicates a generic parsing error that doesn't fit into\n217\t\t// more specific parse error categories. Use this as a fallback when the exact\n218\t\t// nature of the parse error cannot be determined.\n219\t\tErrCodeParseError ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"\n220\t\n221\t\t// ============================================================================\n222\t\t// Validation Error Codes\n223\t\t// ============================================================================\n224\t\n225\t\t// ErrCodeValidationFailed indicates a general validation failure.\n226\t\t// This is used when validation cannot pass but doesn't fit into more specific\n227\t\t// validation error categories.\n228\t\tErrCodeValidationFailed ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"\n229\t\n230\t\t// ErrCodeRequiredField indicates that a required field is missing from the\n231\t\t// YAML data. This error is used when accessing required fields that don't exist\n232\t\t// in the YAML structure, preventing proper configuration or processing.\n233\t\tErrCodeRequiredField ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"\n234\t\n235\t\t// ErrCodeConstraintViolation indicates that a field value violates a defined\n236\t\t// constraint. This includes values out of allowed ranges, string length violations,\n237\t\t// pattern matching failures, or any constraint validation rule defined in the\n238\t\t// schema or validation logic.\n239\t\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\"\n240\t\n241\t\t// ErrCodeInvalidValue indicates that a field contains an invalid value that\n242\t\t// doesn't meet validation criteria. This is used when a value is syntactically\n243\t\t// correct but semantically invalid for the intended use.\n244\t\tErrCodeInvalidValue ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"\n245\t\n246\t\t// ============================================================================\n247\t\t// Schema Error Codes\n248\t\t// ============================================================================\n249\t\n250\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed indicates an error occurred while loading a schema\n251\t\t// definition. This includes file I/O errors reading the schema file, JSON/YAML\n252\t\t// parsing errors in the schema definition itself, or invalid schema syntax.\n253\t\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"\n254\t\n255\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaValidation indicates that YAML data failed validation against\n256\t\t// a schema. This error is returned when the YAML structure or content doesn't\n257\t\t// conform to the rules defined in the validation schema.\n258\t\tErrCodeSchemaValidation ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"\n259\t\n260\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaNotFound indicates that a required schema file cannot be found.\n261\t\t// This error occurs when the schema reference points to a non-existent file or\n262\t\t// the schema cannot be located in the configured schema paths.\n263\t\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"\n264\t\n265\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaInvalid indicates that the schema definition itself is invalid.\n266\t\t// This includes schemas with invalid structure, unsupported features, or schemas\n267\t\t// that cannot be properly parsed or interpreted by the validation system.\n268\t\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"\n269\t)\n270\t\n271\t// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n272\t//\n273\t// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n274\t// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\n275\ttype ParseError struct {\n276\t\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n277\t\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n278\t\tColumn     int         // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n279\t\tMessage    string      // Human-readable error message\n280\t\tContextStr string      // Additional context about the parsing state\n281\t\tErr        error       // Underlying error for error wrapping\n282\t\tErrorType  ErrorType   // Specific type of parse error\n283\t\tErrorCode  ErrorCode   // Error code for programmatic handling\n284\t\tExpected   string      // What was expected (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n285\t\tActual     string      // What was actually found (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n286\t}\n287\t\n288\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n289\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n290\t\tif pe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n291\t\t\treturn pe.ErrorCode\n292\t\t}\n293\t\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n294\t}\n295\t\n296\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n297\tfunc (pe *ParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n298\t\treturn pe.ErrorType\n299\t}\n300\t\n301\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n302\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Context() string {\n303\t\treturn pe.ContextStr\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t// Error implements the error interface.\n307\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Error() string {\n308\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n309\t\n310\t\t// Build base error with location\n311\t\tif pe.Line > 0 {\n312\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s at line %d\", pe.FilePath, pe.Line))\n313\t\t\tif pe.Column > 0 {\n314\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", pe.Column))\n315\t\t\t}\n316\t\t} else {\n317\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s\", pe.FilePath))\n318\t\t}\n319\t\n320\t\t// Add message\n321\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", pe.Message))\n322\t\n323\t\t// Add expected vs actual if available\n324\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" || pe.Actual != \"\" {\n325\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n326\t\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" {\n327\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", pe.Expected))\n328\t\t\t}\n329\t\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" && pe.Actual != \"\" {\n330\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n331\t\t\t}\n332\t\t\tif pe.Actual != \"\" {\n333\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", pe.Actual))\n334\t\t\t}\n335\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n336\t\t}\n337\t\n338\t\treturn sb.String()\n339\t}\n340\t\n341\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n342\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n343\t\treturn pe.Err\n344\t}\n345\t\n346\t// NewParseError creates a new ParseError with the given parameters.\n347\t//\n348\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n349\t// ParseError instances with message, line, column, error code, and optional expected/actual values.\n350\t//\n351\t// Parameters:\n352\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n353\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n354\t//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n355\t//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n356\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n357\t//   - expected: What was expected (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n358\t//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n359\t//\n360\t// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n361\t//\n362\t// Example usage:\n363\t//\n364\t//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\n365\tfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n366\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n367\t\terrorCode := code\n368\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n369\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n370\t\t}\n371\t\n372\t\treturn &ParseError{\n373\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n374\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n375\t\t\tLine:      line,\n376\t\t\tColumn:    column,\n377\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n378\t\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n379\t\t\tExpected:  expected,\n380\t\t\tActual:    actual,\n381\t\t}\n382\t}\n383\t\n384\t// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\n385\tfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n386\t\tvar pe *ParseError\n387\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n388\t}\n389\t\n390\t// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n391\t//\n392\t// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n393\t// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n394\t// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\n395\ttype ValidationError struct {\n396\t\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file being validated\n397\t\tFieldPath    string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n398\t\tPath         string    // Dot-notation field path (e.g., \"spec.replicas\")\n399\t\tMessage      string    // Human-readable error message\n400\t\tLine         int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n401\t\tColumn       int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, optional)\n402\t\tConstraint   string    // Constraint that was violated (optional)\n403\t\tContextStr   string    // Additional context about the validation state (optional)\n404\t\tErr          error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n405\t\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n406\t\tType         ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n407\t\tExpectedType string    // Expected type for type mismatch errors (optional)\n408\t\tActualType   string    // Actual type found for type mismatch errors (optional)\n409\t}\n410\t\n411\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n412\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n413\t\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n414\t\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n415\t\t}\n416\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n417\t}\n418\t\n419\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n420\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n421\t\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n422\t\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n423\t\t\treturn ve.Type\n424\t\t}\n425\t\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n426\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n427\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n428\t\t}\n429\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n430\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n431\t\t}\n432\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n433\t}\n434\t\n435\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n436\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n437\t\treturn ve.ContextStr\n438\t}\n439\t\n440\t// Error implements the error interface.\n441\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n442\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n443\t\n444\t\t// Build base error with location\n445\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n446\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line))\n447\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n448\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n449\t\t\t}\n450\t\t} else {\n451\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", ve.FilePath))\n452\t\t}\n453\t\n454\t\t// Add field path if available\n455\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n456\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at field %s\", ve.FieldPath))\n457\t\t}\n458\t\n459\t\t// Add message\n460\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ve.Message))\n461\t\n462\t\t// Add constraint if available\n463\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n464\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (constraint: %s)\", ve.Constraint))\n465\t\t}\n466\t\n467\t\treturn sb.String()\n468\t}\n469\t\n470\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n471\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n472\t\treturn ve.Err\n473\t}\n474\t\n475\t// String returns a formatted error message with context.\n476\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n477\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n478\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n479\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n480\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n481\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n482\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n483\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n484\t\t\t}\n485\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n486\t\t}\n487\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n488\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n489\t\t}\n490\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n491\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n492\t\t}\n493\t\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n494\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n495\t\t}\n496\t\treturn sb.String()\n497\t}\n498\t\n499\t// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n500\t//\n501\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n502\t// ValidationError instances with message, path, constraint, error code, and location.\n503\t//\n504\t// Parameters:\n505\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being validated\n506\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n507\t//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n508\t//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n509\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n510\t//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n511\t//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n512\t//   - errorType: Category of error (use empty string for default ErrorTypeValidation)\n513\t//   - path: Dot-notation field path (optional, for backward compatibility defaults to empty string)\n514\t//\n515\t// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n516\t//\n517\t// Example usage:\n518\t//\n519\t//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue, 10, 5, \"\", \"spec.replicas\")\n520\tfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath string, constraint string, code ErrorCode, line int, column int, errorType ErrorType, path string) *ValidationError {\n521\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic validation error\n522\t\terrorCode := code\n523\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n524\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeValidationFailed\n525\t\t}\n526\t\n527\t\t// Use provided error type or default to validation error\n528\t\teType := errorType\n529\t\tif eType == \"\" {\n530\t\t\teType = ErrorTypeValidation\n531\t\t}\n532\t\n533\t\treturn &ValidationError{\n534\t\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n535\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n536\t\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n537\t\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n538\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n539\t\t\tLine:       line,\n540\t\t\tColumn:     column,\n541\t\t\tType:       eType,\n542\t\t\tPath:       path,\n543\t\t}\n544\t}\n545\t\n546\t// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\n547\tfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n548\t\tvar ve *ValidationError\n549\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &ve) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\t// NewFileError creates a new FileError with proper initialization.\n553\tfunc NewFileError(path string, operation string, message string, errorCode ErrorCode) *FileError {\n554\t\treturn &FileError{\n555\t\t\tPath:       path,\n556\t\t\tOperation:  operation,\n557\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n558\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n559\t\t}\n560\t}\n561\t\n562\t// NewSchemaValidationError creates a new SchemaValidationError with proper initialization.\n563\tfunc NewSchemaValidationError(filePath string, schemaPath string, fieldPath string, message string, expected string, found string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *SchemaValidationError {\n564\t\treturn &SchemaValidationError{\n565\t\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n566\t\t\tSchemaPath: schemaPath,\n567\t\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n568\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n569\t\t\tExpected:   expected,\n570\t\t\tFound:      found,\n571\t\t\tLine:       line,\n572\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n573\t\t}\n574\t}\n575\t\n576\t// NewTypeMismatchError creates a new TypeMismatchError with proper initialization.\n577\tfunc NewTypeMismatchError(filePath filePath,\n580\t\t\tFieldPath:    fieldPath,\n581\t\t\tExpectedType: expectedType,\n582\t\t\tActualType:   actualType,\n583\t\t\tValue:        value,\n584\t\t\tLine:         line,\n585\t\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n586\t\t}\n587\t}\n588\t\n589\t// NewFieldNotFoundError creates a new FieldNotFoundError with proper initialization.\n590\tfunc NewFieldNotFoundError(filePath string, fieldPath string, errorCode,\n596\t\t}\n597\t}\n598\t\n599\t// NewConstraintError creates a new ConstraintError with proper initialization.\n600\tfunc NewConstraintError(filePath string, fieldPath string, value,\n608\t\t\tLine:           line,\n609\t\t\tErrorCode:      errorCode,\n610\t\t}\n611\t}\n612\t\n613\t// NewSyntaxError creates a new SyntaxError with proper initialization.\n614\tfunc errorCode,\n623\t\t}\n624\t}\n625\t\n626\t// NewStructureError creates a new StructureError with proper initialization.\n627\tfunc NewStructureError(filePath string, message string, line int, line,\n632\t\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n633\t\t\tLocation:     location,\n634\t\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n635\t\t}\n636\t}\n637\t\n638\t// NewDuplicateKeyError creates a new DuplicateKeyError with proper initialization.\n639\t//\n640\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n641\t// DuplicateKeyError instances with the given parameters.\n642\t//\n643\t// Parameters:\n644\t//   - filePath: Path to the file with duplicate keys\n645\t//   - key: The duplicate key name\n646\t//   - location: Nested path to the duplicate key (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n647\t//   - line1: Line number of first occurrence (use 0 if unknown)\n648\t//   - line2: Line number of duplicate occurrence (use 0 if unknown)\n649\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n650\t//\n651\t// Returns a properly initialized DuplicateKeyError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n652\tfunc NewDuplicateKeyError(filePath string, key string, location string, line1 int, line2 int, code ErrorCode) *DuplicateKeyError {\n653\t\t// Use provided code or default to duplicate key error\n654\t\terrorCode := code\n655\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n656\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n657\t\t}\n658\t\n659\t\treturn &DuplicateKeyError{\n660\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n661\t\t\tKey:       key,\n662\t\t\tLocation:  location,\n663\t\t\tLine1:     line1,\n664\t\t\tLine2:     line2,\n665\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n666\t\t}\n667\t}\n668\t\n669\t// NewSchemaLoadError creates a new SchemaLoadError with proper initialization.\n670\t//\n671\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n672\t// SchemaLoadError instances with the given parameters.\n673\t//\n674\t// Parameters:\n675\t//   - filePath: Path to the schema file\n676\t//   - message: Description of the load error\n677\t//   - err: Underlying error (optional, use nil if not applicable)\n678\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n679\t//\n680\t// Returns a properly initialized SchemaLoadError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n681\tfunc NewSchemaLoadError(filePath string, message string, err error, code ErrorCode) *SchemaLoadError {\n682\t\t// Use provided code or default to schema load failed error\n683\t\terrorCode := code\n684\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n685\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed\n686\t\t}\n687\t\n688\t\treturn &SchemaLoadError{\n689\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n690\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n691\t\t\tErr:       err,\n692\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n693\t\t}\n694\t}\n695\t\n696\t// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\n697\tfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n698\t\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n699\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n700\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n701\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n702\t\t\t\treturn true\n703\t\t\t}\n704\t\t}\n705\t\treturn false\n706\t}\n707\t\n708\t// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\n709\tfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n710\t\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n711\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n712\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n713\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n714\t\t\t\treturn true\n715\t\t\t}\n716\t\t}\n717\t\treturn false\n718\t}\n719\t\n720\t// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n721\t//\n722\t// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n723\t// information about the file operation that failed.\n724\ttype FileError struct {\n725\t\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n726\t\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n727\t\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n728\t\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n729\t\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n730\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n731\t}\n732\t\n733\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n734\tfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n735\t\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n736\t\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n737\t\t}\n738\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n739\t}\n740\t\n741\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n742\tfunc (fe *FileError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n743\t\treturn ErrorTypeFile\n744\t}\n745\t\n746\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n747\tfunc (fe *FileError) Context() string {\n748\t\top := fe.Operation\n749\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n750\t\t\top = fe.Op\n751\t\t}\n752\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n753\t}\n754\t\n755\t// Error implements the error interface.\n756\tfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n757\t\top := fe.Operation\n758\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n759\t\t\top = fe.Op\n760\t\t}\n761\t\n762\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n763\t\tif op != \"\" {\n764\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s\", op, fe.Path))\n765\t\t} else {\n766\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s\", fe.Path))\n767\t\t}\n768\t\n769\t\tif fe.Message != \"\" {\n770\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Message))\n771\t\t}\n772\t\n773\t\tif fe.Err != nil {\n774\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Err.Error()))\n775\t\t}\n776\t\n777\t\treturn sb.String()\n778\t}\n779\t\n780\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n781\tfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n782\t\treturn fe.Err\n783\t}\n784\t\n785\t// IsFileError checks if an error is a FileError.\n786\tfunc IsFileError(err error) bool {\n787\t\tvar fe *FileError\n788\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &fe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeFile))\n789\t}\n790\t\n791\t// SyntaxError represents YAML syntax errors during parsing.\n792\t//\n793\t// SyntaxError includes indentation errors, malformed YAML, and other\n794\t// syntax-related issues that prevent the YAML from being parsed.\n795\ttype SyntaxError struct {\n796\t\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with syntax error\n797\t\tLine      int       // Line number where syntax error occurred\n798\t\tColumn    int       // Column number where syntax error occurred\n799\t\tMessage   string    // Description of the syntax error\n800\t\tExpected  string    // What was expected (if known)\n801\t\tFound     string    // What was actually found (if known)\n802\t\tErr       error     // Underlying yaml parser error\n803\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n804\t}\n805\t\n806\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n807\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Code() ErrorCode {\n808\t\tif se.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n809\t\t\treturn se.ErrorCode\n810\t\t}\n811\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n812\t}\n813\t\n814\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n815\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n816\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n817\t}\n818\t\n819\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n820\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Context() string {\n821\t\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", se.Expected)\n822\t\tif se.Found != \"\" {\n823\t\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", found: %s\", se.Found)\n824\t\t}\n825\t\treturn ctx\n826\t}\n827\t\n828\t// Error implements the error interface.\n829\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Error() string {\n830\t\tif se.Line > 0 {\n831\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s at line %d, column %d: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Line, se.Column, se.Message)\n832\t\t}\n833\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Message)\n834\t}\n835\t\n836\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\n837\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Unwrap() error {\n838\t\treturn se.Err\n839\t}\n840\t\n841\t// StructureError represents YAML structure errors during parsing.\n842\t//\n843\t// StructureError includes issues like duplicate keys, invalid nesting,\n844\t// and other structural problems that syntax-checking alone won't catch.\n845\ttype StructureError struct {\n846\t\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file with structure error\n847\t\tLine         int       //     string    // Nested path to the error location\n851\t\tErr          error     // Underlying error\n852\t\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n853\t}\n854\t\n855\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n856\tfunc (ste *StructureError) Code() ErrorCode {\n857\t\tif ste.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n858\t\t\treturn ste.ErrorCode\n859\t\t}\n860\t\tif ste.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n861\t\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n862\t\t}\n863\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n864\t}\n865\t\n866\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n867\tfunc (ste *StructureError) implements the error interface.\n881\tfunc (ste *StructureError) Error() string {\n882\t\tif ste.Line > 0 {\n883\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"structure error in %s at line %d: %s\", ste.FilePath, *StructureError) Unwrap() error {\n890\t\treturn ste.Err\n891\t}\n892\t\n893\t// TypeMismatchError represents type conversion errors during parsing.\n894\t//\n895\t// TypeMismatchError occurs when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n896\t// expected Go type (e.g., trying to parse a string as an integer).\n897\ttype TypeMismatchError struct {\n898\t\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file with type error\n899\t\tFieldPath    string    // Dot-notation path to the field with error\n900\t\tExpectedType string    // Expected type description\n901\t\tActualType   string    // Actual type found\n902\t\tValue        string    // Actual value that caused the error\n903\t\tLine         int       // Line number where error occurred\n904\t\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n905\t}\n906\t\n907\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n908\tfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Code() ErrorCode {\n909\t\tif tme.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n910\t\t\treturn tme.ErrorCode\n911\t\t}\n912\t\treturn ErrCodeTypeMismatch\n913\t}\n914\t\n915\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n916\tfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n917\t\treturn ErrorTypeTypeMismatch\n918\t}\n919\t\n920\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n921\tfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Context() string {\n922\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s, expected type: %s, actual type: %s, value: %s\",\n923\t\t\ttme.FieldPath, tme.ExpectedType, tme.ActualType, tme.Value)\n924\t}\n925\t\n926\t// Error implements the error interface.\n927\tfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Error() string {\n928\t\tif tme.Line > 0 {\n929\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch in %s at line %d, field %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n930\t\t\t\ttme.FilePath, tme.Line, Package yamlutil provides comprehensive error type definitions for YAML processing.\n//\n// Error Hierarchy:\n//\n//\tYAMLError (base interface)\n//\t├── FileError (file I/O errors)\n//\t├── ParseError (YAML parsing errors)\n//\t│   ├── SyntaxError (YAML syntax errors)\n//\t│   ├── StructureError (YAML structure errors)\n//\t│   └── TypeMismatchError (type conversion errors)\n//\t├── ValidationError (validation errors)\n//\t│   ├── FieldNotFoundError (missing required fields)\n//\t│   ├── ConstraintError (constraint violations)\n//\t│   └── DuplicateKeyError (duplicate key errors)\n//\t└── SchemaError (schema-related errors)\n//\t\t├── SchemaLoadError (schema loading errors)\n//\t\t└── SchemaValidationError (schema validation errors)\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// YAMLError is the base interface for all YAML processing errors.\n//\n// This interface provides a common type for all errors that occur during\n// YAML file I/O, parsing, validation, and schema operations.\ntype YAMLError interface {\n\terror\n\n\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic error handling.\n\tCode() ErrorCode\n\n\t// YAMLErrorType returns the category of error for type switching.\n\tYAMLErrorType() ErrorType\n\n\t// Context returns additional context about the error.\n\tContext() string\n}\n\n// ErrorType represents the category of YAML error.\ntype ErrorType string\n\nconst (\n\tErrorTypeFile           ErrorType = \"file\"           // File I/O errors\n\tErrorTypeParse          ErrorType = \"parse\"          // YAML parsing errors\n\tErrorTypeSyntax         ErrorType = \"syntax\"         // YAML syntax errors\n\tErrorTypeStructure      ErrorType = \"structure\"      // YAML structure errors\n\tErrorTypeTypeMismatch   ErrorType = \"type_mismatch\"  // Type conversion errors\n\tErrorTypeValidation     ErrorType = \"validation\"     // Validation errors\n\tErrorTypeFieldNotFound  ErrorType = \"field_not_found\" // Missing required fields\n\tErrorTypeConstraint     ErrorType = \"constraint\"     // Constraint violations\n\tErrorTypeDuplicateKey   ErrorType = \"duplicate_key\"  // Duplicate key errors\n\tErrorTypeSchema         ErrorType = \"schema\"         // Schema-related errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaLoad     ErrorType = \"schema_load\"     // Schema loading errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaValidate ErrorType = \"schema_validate\" // Schema validation errors\n\tErrorTypeUnknown        ErrorType = \"unknown\"        // Unknown error type\n\tErrorTypeEmpty          ErrorType = \"empty\"          // Empty content\n\tErrorTypeIO             ErrorType = \"io\"             // I/O error\n)\n\n// ParseErrorVariant represents the specific variant/category of a parse error.\n//\n// ParseErrorVariant provides a structured way to categorize different types of\n// parse errors, enabling precise error handling and user-friendly error messages.\n// Each variant represents a distinct category of parsing failure.\ntype ParseErrorVariant string\n\nconst (\n\t// ParseErrorVariantSyntax represents YAML syntax errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers fundamental YAML syntax issues including:\n\t// - Invalid indentation\n\t// - Malformed YAML structure\n\t// - Invalid escape sequences\n\t// - Improper quoting\n\t// - Invalid YAML tokens\n\tParseErrorVariantSyntax ParseErrorVariant = \"syntax\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch represents type conversion errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n\t// expected Go type, including:\n\t// - Attempting to parse a string as an integer\n\t// - Trying to unmarshal a scalar into a struct\n\t// - Incompatible array/slice types\n\t// - Invalid boolean representations\n\tParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch ParseErrorVariant = \"type_mismatch\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantValidation represents constraint validation errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors when YAML data violates defined constraints,\n\t// including:\n\t// - Value out of allowed range\n\t// - String length violations\n\t// - Pattern matching failures\n\t// - Required field violations\n\tParseErrorVariantValidation ParseErrorVariant = \"validation\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantIO represents I/O operation errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors related to file operations during parsing,\n\t// including:\n\t// - File not found\n\t// - Permission denied\n\t// - Read/write failures\n\t// - File system errors\n\tParseErrorVariantIO ParseErrorVariant = \"io\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantStructure represents YAML structure errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers structural issues that syntax checking alone won't catch,\n\t// including:\n\t// - Duplicate mapping keys\n\t// - Invalid nesting\n\t// - Circular references\n\t// - Inconsistent document structure\n\tParseErrorVariantStructure ParseErrorVariant = \"structure\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantCustom represents custom/extensible error categories.\n\t//\n\t// This variant is reserved for application-specific parsing errors that don't\n\t// fit into the standard categories. It enables extensibility for domain-specific\n\t// validation rules and custom parsing logic.\n\t// Applications can use this variant with custom error codes and messages\n\t// to represent specialized error conditions.\n\tParseErrorVariantCustom ParseErrorVariant = \"custom\"\n)\n\n// String returns the string representation of the ParseErrorVariant.\nfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) String() string {\n\treturn string(v)\n}\n\n// Description returns a human-readable description of the ParseErrorVariant.\nfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) Description() string {\n\tswitch v {\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantSyntax:\n\t\treturn \"YAML syntax error - invalid YAML structure or formatting\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch:\n\t\treturn \"Type mismatch error - cannot convert YAML to expected type\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Validation error - constraint violation or invalid value\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantIO:\n\t\treturn \"I/O error - file operation failed\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantStructure:\n\t\treturn \"Structure error - invalid YAML document structure\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom error - application-specific parsing error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown parse error variant\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n//\n// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\ntype ErrorCode string\n\nconst (\n\t// ============================================================================\n\t// File Error Codes\n\t// ============================================================================\n\n\t// ErrCodeFileNotFound indicates that a file does not exist at the specified path.\n\t// This error is returned when file operations fail because the target file cannot\n\t// be found in the filesystem.\n\tErrCodeFileNotFound ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeFileAccessDenied indicates insufficient permissions to access a file.\n\t// This error occurs when the application lacks the necessary read or write\n\t// permissions for the specified file path.\n\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeFileIOError indicates a generic file I/O error that doesn't fit\n\t// other specific file error categories. This includes disk errors, filesystem\n\t// issues, or other low-level I/O failures.\n\tErrCodeFileIOError ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeFileEmpty indicates that a file exists but contains no data or\n\t// only whitespace. This error is used when parsing an empty file would result\n\t// in undefined behavior or missing required configuration.\n\tErrCodeFileEmpty ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"\n\n\t// ============================================================================\n\t// Parse Error Codes\n\t// ============================================================================\n\n\t// ErrCodeInvalidSyntax indicates a YAML syntax error during parsing.\n\t// This includes invalid indentation, malformed YAML structure, invalid escape\n\t// sequences, improper quoting, or any fundamental YAML syntax violation that\n\t// prevents the document from being parsed.\n\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeTypeMismatch indicates a type conversion error during parsing.\n\t// This occurs when YAML content cannot be converted to the expected Go type,\n\t// such as attempting to parse a string as an integer, unmarshaling a scalar into\n\t// a struct, or incompatible array/slice types.\n\tErrCodeTypeMismatch ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeInvalidStructure indicates a YAML structure error that passes\n\t// syntax checking but represents invalid document structure. This includes\n\t// invalid nesting, inconsistent document structure, or other structural issues\n\t// that prevent proper interpretation of the YAML data.\n\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeDuplicateKey indicates that a YAML mapping contains the same key\n\t// multiple times. This is ambiguous and often indicates a configuration error,\n\t// as YAML mappings should have unique keys.\n\tErrCodeDuplicateKey ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeParseError indicates a generic parsing error that doesn't fit into\n\t// more specific parse error categories. Use this as a fallback when the exact\n\t// nature of the parse error cannot be determined.\n\tErrCodeParseError ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"\n\n\t// ============================================================================\n\t// Validation Error Codes\n\t// ============================================================================\n\n\t// ErrCodeValidationFailed indicates a general validation failure.\n\t// This is used when validation cannot pass but doesn't fit into more specific\n\t// validation error categories.\n\tErrCodeValidationFailed ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeRequiredField indicates that a required field is missing from the\n\t// YAML data. This error is used when accessing required fields that don't exist\n\t// in the YAML structure, preventing proper configuration or processing.\n\tErrCodeRequiredField ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeConstraintViolation indicates that a field value violates a defined\n\t// constraint. This includes values out of allowed ranges, string length violations,\n\t// pattern matching failures, or any constraint validation rule defined in the\n\t// schema or validation logic.\n\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeInvalidValue indicates that a field contains an invalid value that\n\t// doesn't meet validation criteria. This is used when a value is syntactically\n\t// correct but semantically invalid for the intended use.\n\tErrCodeInvalidValue ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"\n\n\t// ============================================================================\n\t// Schema Error Codes\n\t// ============================================================================\n\n\t// ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed indicates an error occurred while loading a schema\n\t// definition. This includes file I/O errors reading the schema file, JSON/YAML\n\t// parsing errors in the schema definition itself, or invalid schema syntax.\n\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeSchemaValidation indicates that YAML data failed validation against\n\t// a schema. This error is returned when the YAML structure or content doesn't\n\t// conform to the rules defined in the validation schema.\n\tErrCodeSchemaValidation ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeSchemaNotFound indicates that a required schema file cannot be found.\n\t// This error occurs when the schema reference points to a non-existent file or\n\t// the schema cannot be located in the configured schema paths.\n\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeSchemaInvalid indicates that the schema definition itself is invalid.\n\t// This includes schemas with invalid structure, unsupported features, or schemas\n\t// that cannot be properly parsed or interpreted by the validation system.\n\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"\n)\n\n// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n//\n// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\ntype ParseError struct {\n\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int         // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tMessage    string      // Human-readable error message\n\tContextStr string      // Additional context about the parsing state\n\tErr        error       // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorType  ErrorType   // Specific type of parse error\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode   // Error code for programmatic handling\n\tExpected   string      // What was expected (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n\tActual     string      // What was actually found (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif pe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn pe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn pe.ErrorType\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Context() string {\n\treturn pe.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif pe.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s at line %d\", pe.FilePath, pe.Line))\n\t\tif pe.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", pe.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s\", pe.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", pe.Message))\n\n\t// Add expected vs actual if available\n\tif pe.Expected != \"\" || pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", pe.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" && pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", pe.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn pe.Err\n}\n\n// NewParseError creates a new ParseError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// ParseError instances with message, line, column, error code, and optional expected/actual values.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n//   - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//   - expected: What was expected (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\nfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ParseError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\tColumn:    column,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n\t\tExpected:  expected,\n\t\tActual:    actual,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\nfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n\tvar pe *ParseError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n}\n\n// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n//\n// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\ntype ValidationError struct {\n\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file being validated\n\tFieldPath    string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n\tPath         string    // Dot-notation field path (e.g., \"spec.replicas\")\n\tMessage      string    // Human-readable error message\n\tLine         int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn       int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, optional)\n\tConstraint   string    // Constraint that was violated (optional)\n\tContextStr   string    // Additional context about the validation state (optional)\n\tErr          error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n\tType         ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n\tExpectedType string    // Expected type for type mismatch errors (optional)\n\tActualType   string    // Actual type found for type mismatch errors (optional)\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.Type\n\t}\n\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n\t}\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n\t}\n\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n\treturn ve.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", ve.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field path if available\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at field %s\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ve.Message))\n\n\t// Add constraint if available\n\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (constraint: %s)\", ve.Constraint))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ve.Err\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted error message with context.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t}\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n\t}\n\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n\t}\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// ValidationError instances with message, path, constraint, error code, and location.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file being validated\n//   - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - errorType: Category of error (use empty string for default ErrorTypeValidation)\n//   - path: Dot-notation field path (optional, for backward compatibility defaults to empty string)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue, 10, 5, \"\", \"spec.replicas\")\nfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath string, constraint string, code ErrorCode, line int, column int, errorType ErrorType, path string) *ValidationError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to generic validation error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeValidationFailed\n\t}\n\n\t// Use provided error type or default to validation error\n\teType := errorType\n\tif eType == \"\" {\n\t\teType = ErrorTypeValidation\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ValidationError{\n\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t\tLine:       line,\n\t\tColumn:     column,\n\t\tType:       eType,\n\t\tPath:       path,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\nfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n\tvar ve *ValidationError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &ve) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n}\n\n// NewFileError creates a new FileError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewFileError(path string, operation string, message string, errorCode ErrorCode) *FileError {\n\treturn &FileError{\n\t\tPath:       path,\n\t\tOperation:  operation,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSchemaValidationError creates a new SchemaValidationError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewSchemaValidationError(filePath string, schemaPath string, fieldPath string, message string, expected string, found string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *SchemaValidationError {\n\treturn &SchemaValidationError{\n\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n\t\tSchemaPath: schemaPath,\n\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tExpected:   expected,\n\t\tFound:      found,\n\t\tLine:       line,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewTypeMismatchError creates a new TypeMismatchError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewTypeMismatchError(filePath string, fieldPath string, expectedType string, actualType string, value string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *TypeMismatchError {\n\treturn &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\tFilePath:     filePath,\n\t\tFieldPath:    fieldPath,\n\t\tExpectedType: expectedType,\n\t\tActualType:   actualType,\n\t\tValue:        value,\n\t\tLine:         line,\n\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewFieldNotFoundError creates a new FieldNotFoundError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewFieldNotFoundError(filePath string, fieldPath string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *FieldNotFoundError {\n\treturn &FieldNotFoundError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tFieldPath: fieldPath,\n\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewConstraintError creates a new ConstraintError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewConstraintError(filePath string, fieldPath string, constraintType string, constraint string, message string, value string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *ConstraintError {\n\treturn &ConstraintError{\n\t\tFilePath:       filePath,\n\t\tFieldPath:      fieldPath,\n\t\tMessage:        message,\n\t\tConstraintType: constraintType,\n\t\tConstraint:     constraint,\n\t\tValue:          value,\n\t\tLine:           line,\n\t\tErrorCode:      errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSyntaxError creates a new SyntaxError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewSyntaxError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, expected string, found string, errorCode ErrorCode) *SyntaxError {\n\treturn &SyntaxError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\tColumn:    column,\n\t\tExpected:  expected,\n\t\tFound:     found,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewStructureError creates a new StructureError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewStructureError(filePath string, message string, line int, duplicateKey string, location string, errorCode ErrorCode) *StructureError {\n\treturn &StructureError{\n\t\tFilePath:     filePath,\n\t\tMessage:      message,\n\t\tLine:         line,\n\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n\t\tLocation:     location,\n\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewDuplicateKeyError creates a new DuplicateKeyError with proper initialization.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// DuplicateKeyError instances with the given parameters.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file with duplicate keys\n//   - key: The duplicate key name\n//   - location: Nested path to the duplicate key (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//   - line1: Line number of first occurrence (use 0 if unknown)\n//   - line2: Line number of duplicate occurrence (use 0 if unknown)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized DuplicateKeyError that implements the YAMLError interface.\nfunc NewDuplicateKeyError(filePath string, key string, location string, line1 int, line2 int, code ErrorCode) *DuplicateKeyError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to duplicate key error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n\t}\n\n\treturn &DuplicateKeyError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tKey:       key,\n\t\tLocation:  location,\n\t\tLine1:     line1,\n\t\tLine2:     line2,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSchemaLoadError creates a new SchemaLoadError with proper initialization.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// SchemaLoadError instances with the given parameters.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the schema file\n//   - message: Description of the load error\n//   - err: Underlying error (optional, use nil if not applicable)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized SchemaLoadError that implements the YAMLError interface.\nfunc NewSchemaLoadError(filePath string, message string, err error, code ErrorCode) *SchemaLoadError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to schema load failed error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed\n\t}\n\n\treturn &SchemaLoadError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\tErr:       err,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\nfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\nfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n//\n// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n// information about the file operation that failed.\ntype FileError struct {\n\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeFile\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Context() string {\n\top := fe.Operation\n\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n\t\top = fe.Op\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n\top := fe.Operation\n\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n\t\top = fe.Op\n\t}\n\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tif op != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s\", op, fe.Path))\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s\", fe.Path))\n\t}\n\n\tif fe.Message != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Message))\n\t}\n\n\tif fe.Err != nil {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Err.Error()))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn fe.Err\n}\n\n// IsFileError checks if an error is a FileError.\nfunc IsFileError(err error) bool {\n\tvar fe *FileError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &fe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeFile))\n}\n\n// SyntaxError represents YAML syntax errors during parsing.\n//\n// SyntaxError includes indentation errors, malformed YAML, and other\n// syntax-related issues that prevent the YAML from being parsed.\ntype SyntaxError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with syntax error\n\tLine      int       // Line number where syntax error occurred\n\tColumn    int       // Column number where syntax error occurred\n\tMessage   string    // Description of the syntax error\n\tExpected  string    // What was expected (if known)\n\tFound     string    // What was actually found (if known)\n\tErr       error     // Underlying yaml parser error\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif se.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn se.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Context() string {\n\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", se.Expected)\n\tif se.Found != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", found: %s\", se.Found)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Error() string {\n\tif se.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s at line %d, column %d: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Line, se.Column, se.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn se.Err\n}\n\n// StructureError represents YAML structure errors during parsing.\n//\n// StructureError includes issues like duplicate keys, invalid nesting,\n// and other structural problems that syntax-checking alone won't catch.\ntype StructureError struct {\n\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file with structure error\n\tLine         int       // Line number where structure error occurred\n\tMessage      string    // Description of the structure error\n\tDuplicateKey string    // Name of duplicate key (if applicable)\n\tLocation     string    // Nested path to the error location\n\tErr          error     // Underlying error\n\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ste.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ste.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\tif ste.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Context() string {\n\tctx := \"location: \" + ste.Location\n\tif ste.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", duplicate key: %s\", ste.DuplicateKey)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Error() string {\n\tif ste.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"structure error in %s at line %d: %s\", ste.FilePath, ste.Line, ste.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"structure error in %s: %s\", ste.FilePath, ste.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ste.Err\n}\n\n// TypeMismatchError represents type conversion errors during parsing.\n//\n// TypeMismatchError occurs when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n// expected Go type (e.g., trying to parse a string as an integer).\ntype TypeMismatchError struct {\n\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file with type error\n\tFieldPath    string    // Dot-notation path to the field with error\n\tExpectedType string    // Expected type description\n\tActualType   string    // Actual type found\n\tValue        string    // Actual value that caused the error\n\tLine         int       // Line number where error occurred\n\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif tme.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn tme.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeTypeMismatch\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeTypeMismatch\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Context() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s, expected type: %s, actual type: %s, value: %s\",\n\t\ttme.FieldPath, tme.ExpectedType, tme.ActualType, tme.Value)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Error() string {\n\tif tme.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch in %s at line %d, field %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n\t\t\ttme.FilePath, tme.Line, tme.FieldPath, tme.ExpectedType, tme.ActualType)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch in %s, field %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n\t\ttme.FilePath, tme.FieldPath, tme.ExpectedType, tme.ActualType)\n}\n\n// FieldNotFoundError represents an error when a required field is missing.\n//\n// FieldNotFoundError is used when accessing required fields that don't exist\n// in the YAML data structure.\ntype FieldNotFoundError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file missing the field\n\tFieldPath string    // Dot-notation path to the missing field\n\tLine      int       // Line number where field should be (if known)\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fnfe *FieldNotFoundError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif fnfe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fnfe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeRequiredField\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fnfe *FieldNotFoundError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fnfe *FieldNotFoundError) Context() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"required field not found: %s\", fnfe.FieldPath)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (fnfe *FieldNotFoundError) Error() string {\n\tif fnfe.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"required field missing in %s at line %d: %s\", fnfe.FilePath, fnfe.Line, fnfe.FieldPath)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"required field missing in %s: %s\", fnfe.FilePath, fnfe.FieldPath)\n}\n\n// ConstraintError represents constraint validation failures.\n//\n// ConstraintError is used when a field value violates a defined constraint\n// (e.g., value out of range, string too long, pattern mismatch).\ntype ConstraintError struct {\n\tFilePath       string    // Path to the file with constraint error\n\tFieldPath      string    // Dot-notation path to the field\n\tMessage        string    // Human-readable error message\n\tConstraintType string    // Type of constraint (range, length, pattern, etc.)\n\tConstraint     string    // Description of the constraint\n\tValue          string    // Actual value that violated the constraint\n\tLine           int       // Line number where constraint violation occurred\n\tErrorCode      ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ce *ConstraintError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ce.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ce.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeConstraintViolation\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ce *ConstraintError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ce *ConstraintError) Context() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s, constraint: %s, value: %s\",\n\t\tce.FieldPath, ce.Constraint, ce.Value)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ce *ConstraintError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif ce.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"constraint violation in %s at line %d\", ce.FilePath, ce.Line))\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"constraint violation in %s\", ce.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field path if available\n\tif ce.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", field %s\", ce.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message if available, otherwise use constraint\n\tmsg := ce.Message\n\tif msg == \"\" {\n\t\tmsg = ce.Constraint\n\t}\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", msg))\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// DuplicateKeyError represents an error when duplicate keys are found in YAML.\n//\n// DuplicateKeyError occurs when a YAML mapping contains the same key multiple times,\n// which is ambiguous and often indicates a configuration error.\ntype DuplicateKeyError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with duplicate keys\n\tKey       string    // The duplicate key name\n\tLocation  string    // Nested path to the duplicate key\n\tLine1     int       // Line number of first occurrence\n\tLine2     int       // Line number of duplicate occurrence\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif dke.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn dke.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeDuplicateKey\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Context() string {\n\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key: %s\", dke.Key)\n\tif dke.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\" at %s\", dke.Location)\n\t}\n\tif dke.Line1 > 0 && dke.Line2 > 0 {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\" (first at line %d, duplicate at line %d)\", dke.Line1, dke.Line2)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Error() string {\n\tif dke.Line2 > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key error in %s at line %d: key %q already defined at line %d\",\n\t\t\tdke.FilePath, dke.Line2, dke.Key, dke.Line1)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key error in %s: key %q\", dke.FilePath, dke.Key)\n}\n\n// SchemaLoadError represents errors loading schema definitions.\n//\n// SchemaLoadError occurs when a schema file cannot be loaded or parsed.\ntype SchemaLoadError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the schema file\n\tMessage   string    // Description of the load error\n\tErr       error     // Underlying error\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sle *SchemaLoadError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif sle.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn sle.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sle *SchemaLoadError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeSchemaLoad\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sle *SchemaLoadError) Context() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"schema file: %s\", sle.FilePath)\n}\n\n// Error implements (sle *SchemaLoadError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn sle.Err\n}\n\n// SchemaValidationError represents schema validation failures.\n//\n// SchemaValidationError occurs when YAML data fails validation against a schema.\ntype SchemaValidationError struct {\n\tFilePath    string    // Path to the file being validated\n\tSchemaPath  string    // Path to the schema file\n\tFieldPath   string    // was actually found\n\tLine        int       // Line number where validation failed\n\tErrorCode   ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sve *SchemaValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif sve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn sve.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeSchemaValidation\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sve *SchemaValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeSchemaValidate\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sve *SchemaValidationError) error in %s: %s\", sve.FilePath, sve.Message)\n}\n\n// Helper functions for error type checking\n\n// isYAMLErrorOfType checks if an error is a YAMLError of the specified type.\nfunc isYAMLErrorOfType(err error, errorType ErrorType) bool {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to unwrap to get to the base YAMLError\n\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) {\n\t\tif ye, ok := unwrapped.(YAMLError); ok {\n\t\t\treturn ye.YAMLErrorType() == errorType\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// IsYAMLError checks if an error ok\n}\n\n// GetYAMLErrorType returns the ErrorType of a YAMLError, or ErrorTypeUnknown if not a YAMLError.\nfunc GetYAMLErrorType(err error) ErrorType {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to unwrap to get to the base YAMLError\n\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) {\n\t\tif ye, ok := unwrapped.(YAMLError); ok file not found errors.\nfunc IsFileNotFoundError(err error) bool {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if error chain contains os.ErrNotExist\n\tif os.IsNotExist(err) {\n\t\treturn
